hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
household expectations of inflation had firmed
labour market was proving more resilient than expected
greater persistence in domestic wage- and price-setting
dovish_phrases: Underlying UK GDP growth is judged to have slowed
labour market has continued to loosen
Prospects for global growth have weakened
greater or longer-lasting weakness in demand relative to supply
consumer spending remained weak and investment subdued
summary: The MPC voted 5-4 to cut Bank Rate by 25bp to 4.25%, with two members preferring a 50bp cut and two preferring to hold, reflecting continued disinflation progress but with concerns about weaker growth and trade uncertainty partially offset by inflation persistence risks.
